prompt:
The following Lean 4 file defines a first-order rewrite calculus and three proposed termination measures. Assess the three candidates.

Do not modify the file or run the compiler. Do not read any other files in the workspace or search the web.

<workspace>\test-files\Test-05-Candidate-Class-Reasoning.lean
response: None of the three proposed termination measures (mu1, mu2, or mu3) is valid for proving termination of the rewrite system. For each measure, there exist rewrite steps (specifically, applications of the R_rec_succ rule) where the measure does not strictly decrease—in fact, it either stays the same (when the measure of the step argument `s` is 0) or increases (when the measure of `s` is greater than 0). A valid termination measure must strictly decrease on every possible rewrite step to ensure no infinite rewrite chains are possible.

None of the three proposed termination measures (mu1, mu2, or mu3) is valid for proving termination of the rewrite system. For each measure, there exist rewrite steps (specifically, applications of the R_rec_succ rule) where the measure does not strictly decrease—in fact, it either stays the same (when the measure of the step argument `s` is 0) or increases (when the measure of `s` is greater than 0). A valid termination measure must strictly decrease on every possible rewrite step to ensure no infinite rewrite chains are possible.
